steps.title: Compare the new point
steps.content:
Data set A has best-fit line $y=-5.1+8.7x$, so its slope is $8.7$. Check the old model at the new point's $x$-value.
$$-5.1+8.7(9)=73.2$$

steps.title: Predict the slope direction
steps.content: The new point is $(9,52)$, which is far below the old prediction of $73.2$. A far-right point below the old trend pulls the best-fit slope downward, so the new slope should be less than $8.7$.

steps.title: Choose the lower slope
steps.content: Among the choices, only $5.9$ is less than $8.7$ and reasonable for that downward pull. The answer is A.
